content:
# Revelation Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE, page 281 | **Source Image:** REV_005_page_001.jpg

1. And I saw upon the right [hand] of the [one] sitting upon the throne a scroll having been written inside and on the back, having been sealed with seven seals.

2. And I saw a strong angel proclaiming in a great voice, "Who is worthy to open the scroll and to loose the seals of it?"

3. And no one was able in the heaven nor upon the earth nor under the earth to open the scroll nor to look upon it.

4. And I was weeping much, because no one worthy was found to open the scroll nor to look upon it.

5. And one of the elders says to me, "Do not weep; behold, the Lion, the [one] out of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has conquered [so as] to open the scroll and the seven seals of it."

6. And I saw in [the] midst of the throne and of the four living creatures and in [the] midst of the elders a Lamb having stood as having been slain, having seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God having been sent out into all the earth.

7. And he came and has taken [it] out of the right [hand] of the [one] sitting upon the throne.

8. And when he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell before the Lamb, each having a harp and golden bowls being full of incenses, which are the prayers of the holy ones.

9. And they sing a new song, saying, "Worthy are you to take the scroll and to open the seals of it, because you were slain and you purchased for God by the blood of you [those] out of every tribe and tongue and people and nation,

10. and you made them for the God of us a kingdom and priests, and they reign upon the earth."

11. And I saw, and I heard [the] voice of many angels around the throne and of the living creatures and of the elders, and the number of them was myriads of myriads and thousands of thousands,

12. saying in a great voice, "Worthy is the Lamb, the [one] having been slain, to receive the power and riches and wisdom and strength and honor and glory and blessing."

13. And every creature which is in the heaven and upon the earth and under the earth and upon the sea, and all the [things] in them, I heard saying, "To the [one] sitting upon the throne and to the Lamb [be] the blessing and the honor and the glory and the might unto the ages of the ages."

14. And the four living creatures were saying, "Amen." And the elders fell and worshiped.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

- **5:1** — Sinaiticus reads ΒΙΒΛΙΟΝ (scroll/book). The scroll is described as written ΕΣΩΘΕΝ ΚΑΙ ΟΠΙΣΘΕΝ (inside and on the back), indicating an opisthograph.
- **5:5** — Sinaiticus reads ΕΝΙΚΗΣΕΝ (has conquered), aorist indicative active, preserving the completed action.
- **5:6** — Sinaiticus reads ΕΣΤΗΚΟΣ ΩΣ ΕΣΦΑΓΜΕΝΟΝ (having stood as having been slain). The perfect participles convey an ongoing state resulting from past action — the Lamb perpetually bears the marks of slaughter.
- **5:9** — Sinaiticus reads ΗΓΟΡΑΣΑΣ ΤΩ ΘΩ (you purchased for God), without the pronoun "us" (ΗΜΑΣ) that appears in some later manuscripts. This is a significant textual variant: Sinaiticus omits the direct object, reading simply "you purchased for God by your blood out of every tribe..." The Byzantine tradition inserts ΗΜΑΣ (us).
- **5:10** — Sinaiticus reads ΒΑΣΙΛΕΙΑΝ ΚΑΙ ΙΕΡΕΙΣ (a kingdom and priests) rather than ΒΑΣΙΛΕΙΣ ΚΑΙ ΙΕΡΕΙΣ (kings and priests) found in some witnesses. Also reads ΒΑΣΙΛΕΥΟΥΣΙΝ (they reign, present tense) rather than the future ΒΑΣΙΛΕΥΣΟΥΣΙΝ found in other manuscripts.
- **5:14** — Sinaiticus has a shorter ending: the elders fell and worshiped (ΠΡΟΣΕΚΥΝΗΣΑΝ), without the addition "the one living unto the ages of the ages" found in some later manuscripts.

— EMET
